alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study addresses phenomena such as slowing down of training dynamics, crossover between rich and lazy learning, and overfitting in modern neural networks.

alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same list of phenomena and conclusions as in the preprint with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The limitations of mean-field theory are assessed by studying cases with a large but finite number of nodes and training samples.

alignments.rationale: The final paper retains the same limitation assessment as the preprint, with only minor wording adjustments.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using pretrained Resnet50 features and one of our CNN based dimension reduction models, HMMs can classify isolated signs with 90.15% accuracy in the Montalbano dataset using RGB and Skeletal data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using pretrained Resnet50 features and one of our CNN based dimension reduction models, HMMs can classify isolated signs with 90.15% accuracy in the Montalbano dataset using RGB and Skeletal data.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the experimental result identically.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of HMMs is comparable with the current LSTM based models.
alignments.rationale: The performance comparison is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HMMs have fewer parameters and can be trained and run on commodity computers fast, without requiring GPUs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: HMMs have fewer parameters and can be trained and run on commodity computers fast, without requiring GPUs.
alignments.rationale: The claim about computational/resource advantages is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our analysis with deep features shows that HMMs could also be utilized as well as deep sequence models in the challenging isolated sign recognition problem.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our analysis with deep features shows that HMMs could also be utilized as well as deep sequence models in the challenging isolated sign recognition problem.
alignments.rationale: The concluding interpretation about HMM utility relative to deep models remains the same in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed PZRes-Net outperforms state-of-the-art methods significantly in terms of both quantitative metrics and visual quality.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed PZRes-Net framework significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods in hyperspectral image super-resolution.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the high-level performance claim (significant improvement over SOTA) for hyperspectral super-resolution, matching the preprint's result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PZRes-Net improves the PSNR by more than 3dB while saving 2.3 times the parameters and consuming 15 times less FLOPs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PZRes-Net improves the PSNR by more than 3dB compared to existing methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the PSNR improvement but separates out the efficiency claims; the mapping to the PSNR-only publication claim narrows the combined preprint statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PZRes-Net learns a high resolution and zero-centric residual image from both low resolution hyperspectral images and high resolution multispectral images.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims focus on performance metrics and resource savings and do not explicitly restate this architectural/representational description, so it appears omitted from the highlighted publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed quantum algorithm for training nonlinear support vector machines has a running time that scales linearly in the number of training examples.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed quantum algorithm for training nonlinear support vector machines has a running time that scales linearly in the number of training examples.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same complexity scaling claim as the preprint verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Classical SVM-perf achieves linear scaling only for linear SVMs or specific cases of low-rank or shift-invariant kernels.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Classical SVM-perf achieves linear scaling only for linear SVMs or specific cases of low-rank or shift-invariant kernels.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same limitation of classical SVM-perf as presented in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Previously proposed quantum algorithms either have super-linear scaling in the number of training examples or apply to different SVM models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Previously proposed quantum algorithms either have super-linear scaling in the number of training examples or apply to different SVM models that lack desirable properties of the soft-margin SVM model.
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the preprint's critique by adding that the alternate quantum algorithms target models lacking soft-margin SVM properties, clarifying the limitation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm can perform well in practice, not just for asymptotically large data sets.

alignments.rationale: The publication adds that classical simulation provides supporting evidence, which clarifies the claim and increases its evidential support (amplified confidence).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dynamic inconsistencies are shown to be resolved within the framework of quantum decision theory. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dynamic inconsistencies in decision making can be resolved within the framework of quantum decision theory. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The finding is restated with essentially identical meaning and certainty, so it is unchanged.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Quantum decision theory provides an operationally-testable framework for rational choice between alternatives. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This explicit claim that QDT provides an operationally-testable framework is not directly present as a distinct claim in the preprint (it is related to but more strongly stated than P0), so it appears as an added publication-level claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fairness is an important requirement to ensure that Machine Learning predictive systems do not discriminate against specific individuals or sub-populations. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fairness is an important requirement to ensure that Machine Learning predictive systems do not discriminate against specific individuals or sub-populations. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The statement is the same in wording and certainty between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This paper illustrates the subtleties between different notions of fairness through numerous examples and scenarios. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper illustrates the subtleties between different notions of fairness through numerous examples and scenarios. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Wording and certainty are effectively identical, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper addresses which notion of fairness is most suited to a given real-world scenario. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper addresses which notion of fairness is most suited to a given real-world scenario. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The substantive claim is the same; only the label of the claim type changed (contribution vs conclusion) without altering content or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results are summarized in a decision diagram that can assist practitioners and policymakers in navigating the catalog of Machine Learning fairness notions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A decision diagram is provided to help practitioners and policy makers navigate the catalog of Machine Learning fairness notions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both texts describe the same decision-diagram outcome intended to assist practitioners; phrasing and claim strength remain the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ISTNs can be trained adversarially on a classification problem with simulated 2D data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ISTNs can be trained adversarially on a classification problem with simulated 2D data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is restated identically in the publication with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ISTNs improve generalization for age regression and sex classification models when training on data from one site and testing on another. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ISTNs improve generalization for age regression and sex classification models when training on data from one site and testing on another. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the empirical generalization result with the same framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Domain adaptation using ISTNs is conducted at the image-level, allowing for explainable appearance and shape changes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of ISTNs for domain adaptation at the image-level allows for explainable appearance and shape changes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the methodological/conceptual claim with only minor wording changes, preserving meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classical result by Juhani Karhum"aki characterizing intersections of two languages of the form $\text{\{x,y\}}^*\cap \text{\{u,v\}}^*$ has been reformulated and formalized. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The classical result by Juhani Karhumäki characterizing intersections of two languages of the form { x , y } ⁎ ∩ { u , v } ⁎ has been reformulated and formalized. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the reformulation and formalization of the Karhumäki result with equivalent meaning and certainty (differences are orthographic/formatting only).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of morphisms in the reformulation allows for a shorter and more transparent presentation of the result. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of morphisms allows for a shorter and more transparent formulation of the result. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim about morphisms yielding a shorter, more transparent formulation is preserved; minor wording changes do not alter meaning or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The result has been formalized in the proof assistant Isabelle/HOL. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The result has been formalized in the proof assistant Isabelle/HOL. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This implementation/formalization claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The theory of evolutionary computation for discrete search spaces has made significant progress in the last ten years.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The theory of evolutionary computation for discrete search spaces has made significant progress since the early 2010s.
alignments.rationale: The publication rephrases the timeframe more precisely ('since the early 2010s') but conveys the same conclusion; this is a clarification rather than a substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recent theoretical insights on parameter tuning and parameter control have been highlighted.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recent theoretical insights on parameter tuning and parameter control have been highlighted.
alignments.rationale: Wording and emphasis are effectively identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Evolutionary algorithms optimize submodular functions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Evolutionary algorithms optimize submodular functions effectively.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the qualifier 'effectively' and has lower stated certainty (medium), which slightly tempers the original absolute phrasing; overall meaning remains aligned.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The state of the art of drift analysis is presented as one of the most powerful analysis techniques developed in this field.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Drift analysis is one of the most powerful analysis techniques developed in the field of evolutionary computation.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same judgement about drift analysis with similar strength and intent; differences are stylistic.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Forcing the learned reward function to be local Lipschitz-continuous is a sine qua non condition for the method to perform well.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Forcing the learned reward function to be local Lipschitz-continuous is a sine qua non condition for the method to perform well.
alignments.rationale: Claims are identical in content and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The consistent satisfaction of the Lipschitzness constraint on the reward has a strong positive effect on imitation performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The consistent satisfaction of the Lipschitzness constraint on the reward has a strong positive effect on imitation performance.
alignments.rationale: Direct match with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The guarantees derived in this work are valid for any reward satisfying the Lipschitzness condition.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The guarantees derived in this work are valid for any reward satisfying the Lipschitzness condition.
alignments.rationale: Identical claim and level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The generic pessimistic reward preconditioning add-on makes the base method it is plugged into provably more robust.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The generic pessimistic reward preconditioning add-on makes the base method it is plugged into provably more robust.
alignments.rationale: No change in content or confidence between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ANFIS-GOA model was superior to other hybrid models for predicting groundwater level in the first and third piezometers during testing.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ANFIS-GOA model was superior to other hybrid models for predicting groundwater level in multiple piezometers.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hybrid models with optimization algorithms significantly outperformed classical ANN, ANFIS, and SVM models without hybridization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of hybrid models with optimization algorithms was significantly better than that of classical ANN, ANFIS, and SVM models without hybridization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ANFIS-GOA model showed improvements of 14.4%, 3%, 17.8%, and 181% for RMSE, MAE, NSE, and PBIAS in the training stage compared to standalone ANFIS.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ANFIS-GOA model showed improvements of up to 181% in performance metrics compared to standalone ANFIS in the testing stage.
alignments.rationale: The publication alters the specifics (from detailed training-stage metric improvements to a summarized 'up to 181%' in testing), changing the metric focus and evaluation stage and thus revising the original quantitative claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Uncertainty analysis indicated that ANFIS-GOA had the best performance while SVM had the worst among the models evaluated.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Uncertainty analysis indicated that ANFIS-GOA had the best performance while SVM had the worst among the models evaluated.
alignments.rationale: Direct match in content and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GOA enhanced the accuracy of the ANFIS, ANN, and SVM models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: GOA enhanced the accuracy of the ANFIS, ANN, and SVM models.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same conclusion but with higher stated certainty (medium -> high), indicating amplified confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed path integral based graph neural networks (PAN) achieves state-of-the-art performance on various graph classification and regression tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results show that PAN achieves state-of-the-art performance on various graph classification/regression tasks.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same empirical performance claim with comparable strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The diagonal entries of the maximal entropy transition (MET) matrix are directly related to the subgraph centrality, providing a natural and adaptive pooling mechanism.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The diagonal entries of the maximal entropy transition (MET) matrix are directly related to the subgraph centrality, leading to a natural and adaptive pooling mechanism.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PAN generalizes the graph Laplacian to a new transition matrix derived from a path integral formalism.

alignments.rationale: The technical statement about generalizing the graph Laplacian is reframed in the publication as a higher-level claim of versatility and applicability, broadening the framing and presented with stronger certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Most existing GNN architectures can be viewed as special cases of the proposed PAN framework.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Most existing GNN architectures can be viewed as special cases of PAN.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new benchmark dataset from statistical mechanics is proposed to enhance the applications of GNN in physical sciences.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The UMCT-DA model effectively adapts to two pathological organs from the Medical Segmentation Decathlon Datasets under unsupervised domain adaptation settings.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework shows strong potential for real-world applications, even in scenarios where labeled source data is inaccessible.

alignments.pub_claim_text: DiFF-RF overcomes the limitations of the isolation forest (IF) algorithm. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: DiFF-RF significantly improves performance in detecting collective anomalies by considering the frequencies of visits in the leaves of the random trees. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our needle design and calibration approach constitute a very accurate fiber-optical sensor for measuring forces at the needle tip. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed resource-efficient blind quality enhancement (RBQE) approach effectively enhances the quality of compressed images. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiments demonstrate that the RBQE approach achieves state-of-the-art performance in blind quality enhancement and resource efficiency. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Ensemble attacks can effectively evade the enhanced malware detectors, notably downgrading the VirusTotal service. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning techniques can be applied to predict content popularity based on user preferences in next generation edge networks. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The article formulates a comprehensive taxonomy based on machine learning techniques, caching strategies, and edge network characteristics. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We experimentally demonstrate the synthesis of square pulses with different temporal-widths by manufacturing passive diffractive layers that collectively control both the spectral amplitude and the phase of an input terahertz pulse.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our results demonstrate direct pulse shaping in the terahertz spectrum, where the amplitude and phase of the input wavelengths are independently controlled through a passive diffractive device.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the experimental demonstration of amplitude-and-phase control via passive diffractive layers but in more general terms (direct pulse shaping) rather than specifically naming square pulses, so the claim is clarified and broadened.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our results constitute the first demonstration of direct pulse shaping in terahertz spectrum, where a complex-valued spectral modulation function directly acts on terahertz frequencies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our results demonstrate direct pulse shaping in the terahertz spectrum, where the amplitude and phase of the input wavelengths are independently controlled through a passive diffractive device.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the main technical claim of direct pulse shaping but omits the explicit novelty claim (“first demonstration”), so the scientific content is preserved while the novelty assertion is revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A Lego-like physical transfer learning approach is presented to illustrate pulse-width tunability by replacing part of an existing network with newly trained diffractive layers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A physical transfer learning approach illustrates pulse-width tunability by replacing part of an existing network with newly trained diffractive layers.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same physical transfer learning approach and pulse-width tunability claim with equivalent wording and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This learning-based diffractive pulse engineering framework can find broad applications in communications, ultra-fast imaging and spectroscopy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This learning-based diffractive pulse engineering framework can find broad applications in communications, ultra-fast imaging, and spectroscopy.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same application-oriented conclusion with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Real Elliptically Skewed (RESK) distributions integrate robustness and skewness into a unified cluster analysis framework.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Real Elliptically Skewed (RESK) distributions and associated clustering algorithms integrate robustness and skewness into a unified cluster analysis framework.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The RESK distributions generalize the Real Elliptically Symmetric (RES) distributions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Real Elliptically Skewed (RESK) distributions and associated clustering algorithms integrate robustness and skewness into a unified cluster analysis framework.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the integrated-framework aspect rather than explicitly saying "generalize RES," but the intent is the same; wording is clarified/reframed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new robust skew-Huber M-estimator serves as the maximum likelihood estimator for the skew-Huber distribution within the RESK class.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new robust skew-Huber M-estimator serves as the approximate maximum likelihood estimator for the skew-Huber distribution within the RESK class.
alignments.rationale: The publication modifies the estimator claim from an exact MLE to an approximate MLE, which is a substantive tempering of the original statistical claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Numerical experiments demonstrate the usefulness of the proposed methods for analyzing skewed and heavy-tailed data sets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Numerical experiments on simulated and real-world data confirm the usefulness of the proposed methods for skewed and heavy-tailed data sets.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly mentions both simulated and real-world data, clarifying and slightly broadening the evidence base for the numerical demonstrations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Convolutional neural networks (CNN) for image steganalysis demonstrate better performances with employing concepts from high-level vision tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Convolutional neural networks for image steganalysis demonstrate better performances when employing concepts from high-level vision tasks.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BitMix improves the performance of spatial image steganalysis compared to other data augmentation methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed BitMix method improves the performance of spatial image steganalysis compared to other data augmentation methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BitMix generates an embedding adaptive label based on the ratio of the number of pixels modified in the swapped patch to those in the cover-stego pair.
alignments.pub_claim_text: BitMix generates an embedding adaptive label based on the ratio of the number of pixels modified in the swapped patch to those in the cover–stego pair.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same technical description of BitMix's label generation mechanism.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed self-supervised learning framework treats pansharpening as a colorization problem, offering a novel perspective compared to existing methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed self-supervised learning framework treats pansharpening as a colorization problem, offering a novel perspective compared to existing methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this conceptual contribution verbatim, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduction of noise injection by varying downsampling ratios during training addresses the fixed downscale ratio assumption, improving generalization to full-resolution scenarios.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduction of noise injection by varying downsampling ratios during training helps to overcome spatial-detail loss and blur problems.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the effect of noise injection in terms of addressing spatial-detail loss and blur (more specific performance aspects) rather than the broader generalization claim, so the claim is clarified and slightly narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed PanColorization Generative Adversarial Networks (PanColorGAN) framework helps overcome spatial detail loss and blur problems observed in CNN-based pansharpening.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduction of noise injection by varying downsampling ratios during training helps to overcome spatial-detail loss and blur problems.
alignments.rationale: In the publication the mitigation of spatial-detail loss and blur is attributed specifically to the noise-injection training strategy rather than directly to PanColorGAN as a whole, so the attribution and emphasis are revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach outperforms previous CNN-based and traditional methods as demonstrated in experiments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed PanColorization generative adversarial network (PanColorGAN) framework outperforms previous CNN-based and traditional methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Classical approximation tools, such as polynomials or splines, can be encoded as a tensor network with controlled complexity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Besov spaces are continuously embedded into the approximation spaces of tensor networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A scale of interpolated smoothness spaces is continuously embedded into the scale of TT approximation spaces.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Arbitrary Besov functions can be approximated with optimal or near to optimal rate using tensor networks.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An arbitrary function in the approximation class possesses no Besov smoothness unless the depth of the tensor network is limited.
alignments.pub_claim_text: TT approximation spaces are much larger than smoothness spaces when the depth of the tensor network is not restricted.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the depth-dependence result by characterizing TT approximation spaces as much larger than smoothness spaces when depth is unrestricted; this is a clarified formulation of the preprint claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework incorporating a mutable intention filter and a Warp LSTM outperforms baseline approaches in trajectory prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: Both refer to resources and challenges and their role in advancing the field, but the publication rephrases the causal framing and lowers the stated certainty, so the claim is clarified and confidence tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: State-of-the-art methods for visual kinship recognition problems are examined and discussed. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: State-of-the-art methods for visual kinship recognition have been examined and discussed. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The examination of state-of-the-art methods is preserved with essentially identical meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The survey establishes a stronghold for the state of progress in kinship recognition problems. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Kinship recognition has seen significant progress and milestones over the past ten years. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the survey's assessment into a broader statement about the field's progress; the core message about progress is retained but phrased differently, so it is clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Detecting relatives with visual recognition and classifying the relationship has high potential for impact in research and practice. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Detecting relatives through visual recognition has high potential for impact in both research and practical applications. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The impact claim is retained with equivalent meaning and certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: This survey will serve as a central resource for future work in kinship recognition over the next decade. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication asserts a forward-looking role for the survey that was not explicitly stated in the preprint, so this is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Resective surgery may be curative for drug-resistant focal epilepsy, but only 40% to 70% of patients achieve seizure freedom after surgery. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Resective surgery may be curative for drug-resistant focal epilepsy, but only 40% to 70% of patients achieve seizure freedom after surgery. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The sentence is identical between preprint and publication with the same factual content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We developed an algorithm to simulate resections on preoperative MR images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We developed an algorithm to simulate resections on preoperative MR images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Contribution wording is the same in both versions with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model trained on data with manual annotations obtained a median Dice score of 65.3, while the best-performing model trained with no manual annotations achieved a Dice score of 81.7. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model trained on data with manual annotations obtained a median Dice score of 65.3, while the best-performing model trained with no manual annotations achieved a Dice score of 81.7. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Inter-rater agreement between human annotators was 84.0. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Inter-rater agreement between human annotators was 84.0. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The measured value is identical but the reported certainty was reduced from high to medium in the publication, so content is unchanged but confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate a training method for CNNs using simulated resection cavities that can accurately segment real resection cavities, without manual annotations. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate a training method for CNNs using simulated resection cavities that can accurately segment real resection cavities, without manual annotations. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion language is preserved exactly between preprint and publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper presents a novel differential-privacy mechanism for releasing hierarchical counts of individuals. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The optimization model redistributes the noise introduced to achieve differential privacy while meeting consistency constraints between hierarchical levels. (certainty=high, type=result)
